title: IRS Code 599 Meaning (Return Secured) – For Tax Professionals
description: IRS Code 599 typically means the IRS secured a delinquent return for that period (TDI satisfied). Learn what it closes, what it does not confirm, and what to check next.
content:
IRS Transcript Codes • Built for 750,000+ U.S. Tax Professionals

IRS Code 599 Meaning

IRS Code 599 usually means the IRS marked the delinquent-return requirement for that period as satisfied because a return was secured/received. In other words: it closes the TDI (missing return) case for that single module, but it does not automatically mean the return has already been fully processed and assessed. This page explains how to read 599 in context and what to document next.

Try the parser (requires credits)

What is IRS Code 599?

Plain-English definition: IRS Code 599 indicates the IRS secured the return for the delinquent period and treated the missing-return requirement as satisfied for that module.

What it is
TDI satisfying transaction (return secured)
What it closes
That module only
Often paired with
A two-digit closing code
Client-friendly summary
"IRS closed the missing-return case for this period"

When does Code 599 appear?

  • After a delinquent return is secured: The IRS closes the delinquency indicator when a return is obtained/received for that period.
  • While the return is still moving through processing: 599 can appear before you see downstream assessment/posting activity.
  • For one period at a time: One 599 does not clear other delinquent years.

What does Code 599 usually signal?

TDI is satisfied for this module

Professionally, treat 599 as a delinquency workflow milestone: the IRS marked the period as no longer “missing” for TDI purposes.

It does not equal assessment

Do not interpret 599 as “return assessed.” Assessment and processing are shown by other postings. If you need proof the return posted to the account, check for assessment/return-processing codes on the appropriate transcript type.

What should a tax professional do next?

  1. Confirm the exact tax period: Make sure you are looking at the correct module and year.
  2. Look for the closing code: If present, save it in your workpapers (it explains the disposition detail).
  3. Verify posting/assessment status: Confirm whether the return has posted and whether any balance due/refund activity follows.

Example: how this looks in a parsed report

Below is a simplified view of how Code 599 may appear in a delinquency timeline:

Delinquency (TDI) resolution
Code 599 – Return secured (TDI satisfied for this module)
Date: 05/08/2024 • Note: Module satisfied only

FAQ

What does IRS Code 599 mean on a transcript?

It generally indicates the IRS secured the delinquent return and marked the missing-return requirement satisfied for that tax period (module satisfied only).

Does Code 599 mean the return is processed and assessed?

Not necessarily. 599 is a delinquency (TDI) satisfaction indicator. Confirm processing/assessment with other transcript activity.

Why would Code 599 post if the client already filed?

It can post when the IRS resolves the delinquency case based on a secured/received return. Verify the period, filing method, and whether any duplicates or late-file issues exist.

What should I check after Code 599 posts?

Confirm the module is satisfied, check for other delinquent periods, and verify whether the return has posted for processing/assessment. Record any closing code shown.
